revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Ikeda-jinja]][[ja:伊居太神社 (池田市)]] {{Expand Japanese|伊居太神社 (池田市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Ikeda Shrine | native_name = 伊居太神社 (池田市) | image = Ikeda-jinja, keidai.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Ikeda Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|34.82963|135.425557}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | website = {{Official website|http://www.ikeda-jinjya.jp/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Ikeda Shrine'''|伊居太神社 (池田市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Settsu Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160338|ltq=Q135160338|qid=Q135160338|1=Kanpei-sha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11498550|ltq=Q11498550|qid=Q11498550|1=List of Shikinaisha in Settsu Province}} ** Series ordinal: 43 ** P155: {{ill|Q11675602|ltq=Q11675602|qid=Q11675602|1=Kamo Shrine (Kawanishi)}} ** P156: {{ill|Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)|lt=Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)|ja|多太神社 (川西市)|qid=Q48757073|1=Tada Shrine (Tabuto Shrine)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q817965|ltq=Q817965|qid=Q817965|1=Ikeda, Osaka}} * {{ill|Settsu Province|lt=Settsu Province|ca|Província de Settsu|cs|Provincie Seccu|de|Provinz Settsu|en|Settsu Province|eo|Provinco Seccu|es|Provincia de Settsu|fa|ولایت ستسو|fr|Province de Settsu|gl|Provincia de Settsu|hak|Settsu-koet|id|Provinsi Settsu|it|Settsu (provincia)|ja|摂津国|ko|셋쓰국|lt|Seccu žemė|nl|Settsu (provincie)|pt|Província de Settsu|ru|Сэтцу (провинция)|simple|Settsu Province|sk|Seccu (provincia)|sr|Провинција Сецу|tr|Settsu (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Сеццу|zh_min_nan|Settu-kok|zh_yue|攝津國|zh|攝津國|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} * {{ill|Q1042053|ltq=Q1042053|qid=Q1042053|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|34.82963|135.425557}} == official website (P856) == * http://www.ikeda-jinjya.jp/ == image (P18) == * Ikeda-jinja, keidai.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181064 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} * 181063 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Son-sha|lt=Son-sha|ja|村社|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * いけだじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 2120905003199 == Commons category (P373) == * Ikeda-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Iketano Shrine|lt=Iketano Shrine|qid=Q135039219|1=Iketano Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '大阪府池田市綾羽2丁目4-5', 'language': 'ja'} == Japan Search name ID (P6698) == * 伊居太神社(穴織社) {{commons category|Category:Ikeda-jinja}} [[JA:伊居太神社 (池田市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11379329}} == Japanese content == {{Shrine |Name=Ikeda Shrine |Image=[[File:Ikeda-jinja, keidai.jpg|280px]]
Shrine grounds |Location={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikeda City|Q817965|qid=Q817965|1=Ikeda, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Aya (Ikeda City)|Q86737731|qid=Q86737731|lt=Aya|1=Aya}} 2-chome 4-5 |Coordinates={{WikiCoordinates2段DMS|34|49|48.7|N|135|25|30.7|E|region:JP}} |Enshrined deity=Anaori Daimyōjin
{{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}
{{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} |Founded={{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} year 77 |Shrine rank={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Main hall=Sansha-zukuri |Annual festival={{ill|lt=October 17|Q2962|qid=Q2962|1=October 17}} |Map={{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Ikeda Shrine|lat_deg=34|lat_min=49|lat_sec=48.7|lon_deg=135|lon_min=25|lon_sec=30.7|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|marker=religious-shinto|coord={{coord|34|49|48.7|N|135|25|30.7|E}}}} }} '''Ikeda Shrine''' (いけだじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|qid=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ikeda City|Q817965|qid=Q817965|1=Ikeda, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Aya (Ikeda City)|Q86737731|qid=Q86737731|lt=Aya|1=Aya}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|lt=former shrine rank|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} it was classified as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. It is also known as “Anaori-no-miya Ikeda Shrine.” === History === During the reign of {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Achi no Omi|Q11657596|qid=Q11657596|1=Achi no Omi}} and {{ill|lt=Tsuga no Omi|Q24863564|qid=Q24863564|1=Tsuga no Omi}}, a father and son who were dispatched to the state of {{ill|lt=Wu|Q49696|qid=Q49696|lt=Wu|1=Northern and Southern dynasties}} (referring to the {{ill|lt=Southern Dynasties|Q49696|qid=Q49696|lt=Southern Dynasties|1=Northern and Southern dynasties}} of {{ill|lt=China|Q29520|qid=Q29520|1=China (region)}}{{Cite news|author={{ill|lt=Masatoshi Ishii|Q11585005|qid=Q11585005|1=Masatoshi Ishii}}|date=2005-06|title=Japan–Korea Relations in the 5th Century – The Five Kings of Wa and Goguryeo and Baekje –|publisher={{ill|lt=Japan–Korea Joint History Research|Q11510754|qid=Q11510754|1=Japan–South Korea Joint History Research Project}}|newspaper=Japan–Korea Joint History Research Report (Phase 1)|url=http://www.jkcf.or.jp/history_arch/first/1/1-03-ishii_j.pdf|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20151018092747/http://www.jkcf.or.jp/history_arch/first/1/1-03-ishii_j.pdf|archivedate=2015-10-18|page=147}}), in order to acquire weaving and sewing techniques, returned after requesting from the King of Wu four seamstresses: {{ill|lt=Kureori|Q55525775|qid=Q55525775|1=Kurehatori}}, {{ill|lt=Anaori|Q119380538|qid=Q119380538|1=Anaori}}, {{ill|lt=E-hime|Q119380627|qid=Q119380627|1=Ehime}}, and {{ill|lt=Oto-hime|Q119380628|qid=Q119380628|1=Otohime}}. Of these, the younger sister Anaori-hime of the Kureori and Anaori sisters, who had been welcomed to the Ikeda area, died in {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}}’s 76th year, and it is said that she was enshrined the following year, in the 77th year, by Emperor Nintoku. It is considered one of the candidate shrines (ronsha) for the Ikeda Shrine of {{ill|lt=Kawabe District|Q1042053|qid=Q1042053|lt=Kawabe District|1=Kawabe District, Hyōgo}} in {{ill|lt=Settsu Province|Q864163|qid=Q864163|1=Settsu Province}} listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. The other candidate shrine is {{ill|lt=Ikeda Shrine (Amagasaki)|Q11379327|qid=Q11379327|lt=Ikeda Shrine|1=Ikota Shrine}} (Ikota Shrine) in {{ill|lt=Amagasaki City|Q213318|qid=Q213318|1=Amagasaki}}, {{ill|lt=Hyōgo Prefecture|Q130290|qid=Q130290|1=Hyōgo Prefecture}}. In {{ill|lt=Enryaku|Q1373176|qid=Q1373176|1=Enryaku}} 4 ({{ill|lt=785|Q30554|qid=Q30554|1=785}}), by imperial order of {{ill|lt=Emperor Kanmu|Q314846|qid=Q314846|1=Emperor Kanmu}}, the shrine buildings were newly constructed, and Emperor Ōjin and Emperor Nintoku also came to be enshrined. In {{ill|lt=Tenshō|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 7 ({{ill|lt=1579|Q6709|qid=Q6709|1=1579}}), the shrine buildings were destroyed by fire in the warfare between {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} and {{ill|lt=Araki Murashige|Q2859472|qid=Q2859472|1=Araki Murashige}} during the {{ill|lt=Siege of Arioka Castle|Q7510108|qid=Q7510108|1=Siege of Itami (1579)}}. Later, in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1604|Q6738|qid=Q6738|1=1604}}), the main hall was rebuilt by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|qid=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}}. In the {{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} period, it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Principal deities – Anaori Daimyōjin, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ōjin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Nintoku|Q313119|qid=Q313119|1=Emperor Nintoku}} === Shrine grounds === * Main hall – Rebuilt in {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 9 ({{ill|lt=1604|Q6738|qid=Q6738|1=1604}}) by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|qid=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}}. It is the only example in Japan of a three-shrine (sansha-zukuri) structure with a chidori karahafu hipped roof. * Middle gate * Worship hall * Yorimitsu Pine – A pine tree planted by {{ill|lt=Minamoto no Yorimitsu|Q590523|qid=Q590523|1=Minamoto no Yorimitsu}}. * Himemuro mound * Shrine office * Shrine gate File:Ikeda-jinja, nakamon.jpg|Main hall and middle gate File:Ikeda-jinja, haiden.jpg|Shrine gate File:Ikeda-jinja, torii.jpg|Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|qid=Q687168|lt=Ryōkō Daijingū|1=Ise Grand Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Matsuo Taisha|Q710302|qid=Q710302|lt=Matsuo Shrine|1=Matsunoo Taisha}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q478504|lt=Kunitokotachi no Mikoto|1=Kuni-no-Tokotachi}} Shrine * {{ill|lt=Sumiyoshi Taisha|Q705949|qid=Q705949|lt=Sumiyoshi Shrine|1=Sumiyoshi-taisha}} * Inatsuhiko Shrine – Enshrined deities: Achi no Omi, Tsuga no Omi. * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Shrine|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|Q191763|qid=Q191763|lt=Itsukushima Shrine|1=Itsukushima Shrine}} File:Ikeda-jinja, Inatsuhiko-jinja.jpg|Inatsuhiko Shrine, a subsidiary shrine within the grounds === Festivals === * New Year’s Day Festival ({{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=New Year’s Day|1=UNKNOWN}}) * New Year’s Eve Festival ({{ill|lt=New Year’s Eve|Q2357104|qid=Q2357104|1=Ōmisoka}}) === Cultural properties === === Ikeda City–designated tangible cultural property === * Illustrated map of Ikeda Village, Toyoshima District, Settsu Province, Genroku 10 (historical material) – Designated on March 24, {{ill|lt=2003|Q1986|qid=Q1986|1=2003}} ({{ill|lt=Heisei|Q234510|qid=Q234510|1=Heisei era}} 15)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of Designated Cultural Properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Ikeda City file from the Osaka Prefectural website, updated November 21, Heisei 30).. === Other === * Triangular-rimmed deity-and-beast mirror *: A mirror handed down as a treasure of Ikeda Shrine. It is said to be a Chinese-made mirror and measures 20.5 centimeters in diameter. It is said to have been unearthed from Yokoyama (Yokikiyama) during the {{ill|lt=Kyōwa|Q960763|qid=Q960763|1=Kyōwa}} era ({{ill|lt=1801|Q6877|qid=Q6877|1=1801}}–{{ill|lt=1803|Q6887|qid=Q6887|1=1803}}), but the specific site of excavation (tumulus) is not known“Newly Revised History of Ikeda City, Vol. 1 (Geography, Archaeology, Ancient and Medieval History),” Ikeda City, 1997, pp. 187–188.. === Other === * Also written as “Anaori-hime” or “Ayaori-hime.” * “Ayaori-hime” is the origin of the town name “Aya” in {{ill|lt=Ikeda City|Q817965|qid=Q817965|1=Ikeda, Osaka}}. * It is located right next to {{ill|lt=Satsukiyama Park|Q11372461|qid=Q11372461|1=Satsukiyama-park}} and {{ill|lt=Ikeda Municipal Satsukiyama Zoo|Q11551834|qid=Q11551834|lt=Satsukiyama Zoo|1=Satsukiyama Zoo}}, forming an almost continuous area. * It is the oldest extant {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ikeda City. * While Gofuku-hime is enshrined at {{ill|lt=Gofuku Shrine|Q11416775|qid=Q11416775|1=Kureha Shrine}}, located on the {{ill|lt=Ikeda Station|Q866156|qid=Q866156|lt=Ikeda Station|1=Ikeda Station (Osaka)}} side and regarded as the “Lower Shrine,” Ikeda Shrine at the foot of {{ill|lt=Mount Satsukiyama|Q11372460|qid=Q11372460|1=Mount Satsuki}} is regarded as the “Upper Shrine,” and the relationship between the two is very close. * Locally, it is sometimes called “Ikota” Shrine. === Access === * 15-minute walk from {{ill|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line|Q3832859|qid=Q3832859|lt=Hankyu Takarazuka Line|1=Hankyu Takarazuka Main Line}} / {{ill|lt=Ikeda Station|Q866156|qid=Q866156|lt=Ikeda Station|1=Ikeda Station (Osaka)}}. * 5-minute walk from Aya Town via {{ill|lt=Hankyu Bus|Q11656745|qid=Q11656745|1=Hankyu Bus}}. === Notes === {{Notes help}} {{Reflist}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Ikeda-jinja}} * [http://www.ikeda-jinjya.jp/ Anaori-no-miya Ikeda Shrine] – Official website {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ikeda Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ikeda Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Ikeda, Osaka]] [[Category:池田市の歴史]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Settsu Province]] [[Category:Son-sha in Osaka Prefecture]]
